extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won from GAVIRIA wheel after VAN POPPEL Danny leadout, surviving late KRISTOFF Alexander charge for fifth title.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe controlled with BENNETT but KRISTOFF positioned independently, showing dual-threat sprinter strategy rather than single leadout train.
REIJNDERS Jens survived in two sequential breakaways including regaining contact after Mammolshain drop, revealing exceptional diesel endurance pattern.
Finale attacks on Mammolshain failed to stick before 50km, suggesting sprinter teams now defend earlier than traditional final-lap control.
DOWSETT Alex stringing out late for Israel-Premier suggests non-sprint teams gambling on attrition rather than accepting bunch kick.

rider_signals.REIJNDERS Jens: positive: attacked twice, regained contact after being dropped on Mammolshain, caught only inside final kilometers shows exceptional breakaway durability
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: returned to top form after difficult season, won cleanly from second wheel behind VAN POPPEL
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: positioned himself independently from BENNETT despite same team, nearly won fifth title suggesting protected co-leadership still viable
rider_signals.BINIAM Girmay: neutral: animated late but faded, first race back after Giro suggests timing still off
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: executed textbook leadout into headwind finale, could celebrate 200m out showing total control
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: neutral: fourth with home crowd suggests still ceiling below top-tier sprinters despite Bahrain support
